title: Zeuxine longilabris
taxonomy: Zeuxine longilabris is classified in the family Orchidaceae. This orchid is a species of orchid that belongs to the genus Zeuxine. Zeuxine longilabris is native to Asia, where it can be found growing in various environments. The classification of Zeuxine longilabris is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Monocots, order Asparagales, family Orchidaceae, genus Zeuxine, species Z. longilabris. Further classification includes the species being divided into two subspecies, Z. longilabris subsp. longilabris and Z. longilabris subsp. borneensis.

intro: Zeuxine longilabris is a species of orchid, specifically a type of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Zeuxine longilabris include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Zeuxine longilabris is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory infections, skin conditions, and other health issues.
